scope: Frontend / Canvas portion for DevDay Hackathon 2026

implementedCards.title: Integrate tldraw v3 canvas
implementedCards.notes: Canvas supports sticky notes, freehand draw, shapes, text, native tldraw selection, pan, zoom, and independently addressable shape IDs.

implementedCards.title: Connect tldraw to sync client
implementedCards.notes: Implemented a custom WebSocket sync protocol around tldraw store deltas. This intentionally does not use @tldraw/sync or y-websocket because the hackathon disallows prebuilt websocket sync.

implementedCards.title: Cursor presence with user identity
implementedCards.notes: Presence sends cursor x/y plus name, color, role, and sessionId over the custom WebSocket channel.

implementedCards.title: Task board side panel UI
implementedCards.notes: Right-side collapsible panel renders tasks from a Yjs Y.Array. Task rows include title, author avatar, role, timestamp, and click-to-zoom back to the originating tldraw node with a highlight pulse.

implementedCards.title: Node intent badge animation
implementedCards.notes: Framer Motion badges render on node corners for action, decision, question, and reference intent. Current classification is a frontend heuristic placeholder for the future AI service.

implementedCards.title: RBAC UI affordances
implementedCards.notes: Selected nodes can be locked to Lead or Contributor roles. Client guards mutations and the custom WebSocket server validates incoming shape deltas against node metadata.

implementedCards.title: Visual polish
implementedCards.notes: Consistent work-focused palette, typography, spacing, panels, buttons, avatars, badges, and replay controls.

implementedCards.title: Responsive layout (1280px to 1920px)
implementedCards.notes: Main layout avoids horizontal scroll at standard widths. The execution panel collapses to an icon/count rail at narrower desktop widths.

implementedCards.title: Time travel replay UI
implementedCards.notes: Bottom replay bar shows event count, timestamps, scrubber, play/pause, and speed controls. Scrubbing previews historical canvas frames in read-only mode and restores the live canvas on release.

implementedCards.title: Onboarding tooltips for canvas usability
implementedCards.notes: First-time 3-step tour covers Add sticky, Drag to move, and Type to create task. Completion is stored in localStorage.

routes.name: Canvas room
routes.description: Loads a Ligma canvas room. The room query param is normalized and used for the WebSocket sync room.

messagesClientToServer.description: Joins a room and requests missed events after lastEventSeq.
Sends tldraw store shape deltas only, not full canvas state.
Sends cursor coordinates relative to the canvas stage. Identity comes from the hello session.
Sends encoded Yjs updates for the shared task Y.Array projection.

messagesServerToClient.description: Hydrates current room state, missed append-only events, Yjs task state, and active users.
Broadcasts accepted shape deltas plus authoritative append-only event entries.
Returned when server-side node RBAC rejects a shape mutation.
Broadcast cursor presence for another connected client.

taskEntry.duplicationRule: Tasks are projections that keep nodeId as the source-of-truth link back to the canvas node.

appendOnlyEvent.reconnectRule: Client sends lastEventSeq in hello; server returns events with seq greater than that value.

integrationNotes: AI classification is not implemented here. The current frontend heuristic exists only to drive badges and task-board UI until the AI service provides intent metadata.
Future AI/backend integration can update TLShape.meta.ligma.intent or replace classifyIntent in src/App.tsx with a service-driven classifier.
Server-side RBAC validation currently applies to custom websocket shape deltas. Any future REST or persistence layer must reuse the same node-level lockedToRoles contract.
The sync server is in-memory for hackathon demo speed. Persist rooms/events before production deployment.
